    nixpkgs.url = "github:meta-introspector/nixpkgs?ref=feature/CRQ-016-nixify";

      inputs.nixpkgs.follows = "nixpkgs"; # Ensure consistency
  outputs = { self, nixpkgs, flake-utils, crq-binstore, geminiCliFlake }:

        pkgs = nixpkgs.legacyPackages.${system};
        defaultNarFileName = "llm-context-OEIS-latest.nar";
        mkLlmTask = { narFileName ? defaultNarFileName, promptTemplate ? defaultPromptTemplate, llmModel ? defaultLlmModel }:
            pname = "llm-task-${narFileName}";
            buildInputs = [ pkgs.nix pkgs.bash pkgs.jq ]; # Added pkgs.jq
            CRQ_BINSTORE_PATH = crq-binstore;
            NAR_FILE_NAME = narFileName;
            # In a more complex scenario, we might have a separate script file
                            # Create the fetch-nar-data.sh script within the derivation
                            cat > $out/bin/fetch-nar-data.sh << EOF
              OUTPUT_DIR="$out/unpacked-nar-data" # Unpack directly into the output
              nix-nar-unpack --file "''${NAR_PATH}" --to "''${OUTPUT_DIR}"
                            chmod +x $out/bin/fetch-nar-data.sh
              $out/bin/fetch-nar-data.sh
              UNPACKED_DATA_DIR="$out/unpacked-nar-data"

Processing file: ./09/26/synapse-system/vendor/nix/dream2nix/dev-flake/flake-compat.nix
# copied from github:nix-community/flake-compat/pull/4/head
# Compatibility function to allow flakes to be used by
# non-flake-enabled Nix versions. Given a source tree containing a
# 'flake.nix' and 'flake.lock' file, it fetches the flake inputs and
# calls the flake's 'outputs' function. It then returns an attrset
# containing 'defaultNix' (to be used in 'default.nix'), 'shellNix'
# (to be used in 'shell.nix').
    lockFilePath = src + "/flake.lock";
            {url = "https://api.${info.host or "github.com"}/repos/${info.owner}/${info.repo}/tarball/${info.rev}";}
              if info ? narHash
              then {sha256 = info.narHash;}
        inherit (info) narHash;
                inherit (info) url;
          inherit (info) narHash;
      else if info.type == "path"
        outPath = builtins.path {
          path =
            if builtins.substring 0 1 info.path != "/"
            then src + ("/" + info.path)
            else info.path;
        inherit (info) narHash;
            {inherit (info) url;}
              if info ? narHash
              then {sha256 = info.narHash;}
        inherit (info) rev narHash lastModified;
            {url = "https://${info.host or "gitlab.com"}/api/v4/projects/${info.owner}%2F${info.repo}/repository/archive.tar.gz?sha=${info.rev}";}
              if info ? narHash
              then {sha256 = info.narHash;}
        throw "flake input has unsupported input type '${info.type}'";
    callLocklessFlake = flakeSrc: let
      flake = import (flakeSrc + "/flake.nix");
      outputs = flakeSrc // (flake.outputs {self = outputs;});
      isGit = builtins.pathExists (src + "/.git");
      isShallow = builtins.pathExists (src + "/.git/shallow");
    # Format number of seconds in the Unix epoch as %Y%m%d%H%M%S.
              type = "path";
              narHash = throw "narHash unimplemented for impureOverride";
          flake = import (sourceInfo
            + "/flake.nix");
          # either a node name, or a 'follows' path from the root
          # Follow an input path (e.g. ["dwarffs" "nixpkgs"]) from the
          getInputByPath = nodeName: path:
            if path == []
              (resolveInput lockFile.nodes.${nodeName}.inputs.${builtins.head path})
              (builtins.tail path);
          outputs = flake.outputs (inputs // {self = result;});
              _type = "flake";
          if node.flake or true
          then assert builtins.isFunction flake.outputs; result
      if !(builtins.pathExists lockFilePath)

    nixpkgs.follows = "dream2nix/nixpkgs";
    dream2nix,
    nixpkgs,
    eachSystem = nixpkgs.lib.genAttrs [
      # by passing in our desired nixpkgs revision plus
      # any dream2nix modules needed by it.
      default = dream2nix.lib.evalModules {
        packageSets.nixpkgs = nixpkgs.legacyPackages.${system};
          # Import our actual package definiton as a dream2nix module from ./default.nix
          ./default.nix
            # Aid dream2nix to find the project root. This setup should also works for mono
                      paths = {
                        # can be changed to ".git" or "flake.nix" to get rid of .project-root
                        projectRootFile = "flake.nix";

Processing file: ./09/26/synapse-system/vendor/nix/dream2nix/lib/internal/nodejsUtils.nix
  parseSpdxId,
    {license = parseSpdxId (packageJson.license or "");}
    # returns the parsed package-lock.json for a given project
  identifyGitUrl = url:
    l.hasPrefix "git+" url
    || l.match ''^github:.*/.*#.*'' url != null;
  parseGitUrl = url: let
    githubMatch = l.match ''^github:(.*)/(.*)#(.*)$'' url;
      url = "https://github.com/${owner}/${repo}";
      splitUrlRev = l.splitString "#" url;
      urlOnly = l.head splitUrlRev;
      if l.hasPrefix "git+ssh://" urlOnly
        url = "https://${(l.last (l.splitString "@" url))}";
      else if l.hasPrefix "git+https://" urlOnly
        url = l.removePrefix "git+" urlOnly;
      else throw "Cannot parse git url: ${url}";
Processing file: ./09/26/synapse-system/vendor/nix/dream2nix/lib/internal/parseSpdxId.nix

Processing file: ./09/26/synapse-system/vendor/nix/dream2nix/modules/dream2nix/core/deps/default.nix
        Nothing will stop users from adding `nixpkgs` itself as a dependency, but this will make it very hard for the user of the package to override any dependencies, because they'd have to figure out a way to insert their changes into the Nixpkgs fixpoint. By adding specific attributes to `deps` instead, the user has a realistic chance of overriding those dependencies.
        So deps should be specific, but not overly specific. For instance, the caller shouldn't have to know the version of a dependency in order to override it. The name should suffice. (e.g. `nix = nixVersions.nix_2_12` instead of `inherit (nixVersions) nix_2_12`.
        {nixpkgs, ...}: {
          inherit (nixpkgs) stdenv;
          inherit (nixpkgs.haskellPackages) pandoc;

      packageSets.nixpkgs

  parseSource = plent:
        url = l.removePrefix "git+" (builtins.head split);
      fetchurl {
        url = plent.resolved;
  getDependencies = lock: path: plent:
        packageIdent = nodejsLockUtils.findEntry lock path name;
  parseEntry = lock: path: entry:
    if path == ""
          dependencies = getDependencies lock path entry;
      source = parseSource entry;
        name = l.last (builtins.split "node_modules/" path);
            dependencies = getDependencies lock path entry;

    (l.mapAttrsToList (parseEntry lock) lock.packages);

Processing file: ./09/26/synapse-system/vendor/nix/dream2nix/modules/dream2nix/rust-cargo-lock/translate.nix
  parseSpdxId,
    # this is used while resolving path dependencies
      (import ./findAllCrates.nix {inherit lib;})
    # and while resolving path dependencies
      # Filter root referencing member, we already parsed this (rootToml)
      # Note: the ordering is important here, since packageToml assumes
    parsedLock = projectTree.files."Cargo.lock".tomlContent;
    parsedDeps = parsedLock.package;
    getChecksum = dep: parsedLock.metadata."checksum ${makeDepId dep}";
      parsedDeps;
    # takes a parsed dependency entry and finds the original dependency from `Cargo.lock`
    # This parses a "package-name version (source)" entry in the "dependencies"
    parseDepEntryImpl = entry: let
      parsed = l.splitString " " entry;
      name = l.head parsed;
      # parse the version if it exists
        if l.length parsed > 1
        then l.elemAt parsed 1
      # parse the source if it exists
        if l.length parsed > 2
        then l.removePrefix "(" (l.removeSuffix ")" (l.elemAt parsed 2))
      makePair = entry: l.nameValuePair entry (parseDepEntryImpl entry);
      depEntries = l.flatten (l.map (dep: dep.dependencies or []) parsedDeps);
    parseDepEntry = entry: entryToDependencyAttrs.${entry};
    # Parses a git source, taken straight from nixpkgs.
    parseSourceImpl = src: let
            url = l.elemAt parts 0;
    parsedSources = l.listToAttrs (
      (dep: l.nameValuePair dep.source (parseSourceImpl dep.source))
      (l.filter (dep: dep ? source) parsedDeps)
    parseSource = dep:
      then parsedSources.${dep.source}
        type = "path";
      source = parseSource rawObj;
      if l.length duplicateVersions > 1 && source.type != "path"
      # relative path of the project within the source tree.
          # function to find path replacements for one package
            # We only need to patch path dependencies
            pathDeps = l.filter (dep: dep ? path) tomlDeps;
            # filter out path dependencies whose path are same as in workspace members.
            # this is because otherwise workspace.members paths will also get replaced in the build.
                  !(l.any (memberPath: dep.path == memberPath) workspaceMembers)
              pathDeps;
              name = dep.path;
              value = sanitizeRelativePath "${package.relPath}/${dep.path}";
            # this means that the path doesn't need to be replaced because it's
                n: v: ! l.pathExists (sanitizePath "${projectSource}/${v}")
          # find replacements for all packages we export
            (l.map parseSource parsedDeps)
                {license = parseSpdxId licenseRaw;}
      exportedPackages = let
      serializedRawObjects = parsedDeps;
          (l.map parseDepEntry (rawObj.dependencies or []));
          source = parseSource rawObj;
            path = dependencyObject: let
                  type = "path";
                  path = projectRelPath;
                  type = "path";
                  path = workspaceCrate.relPath;
                  type = "path";
                  path = nonWorkspaceCrate.relPath;
              parsed = source.value;
                if parsed.type or null == "branch"
                then {ref = "refs/heads/${parsed.value}";}
                else if parsed.type or null == "tag"
                then {ref = "refs/tags/${parsed.value}";}
                inherit (parsed) url;
                rev = parsed.sha;

Processing file: ./09/26/synapse-system/vendor/nix/pip2nix/default.nix
{ pkgs ? import ./nix {}
, sources ? import ./nix/sources.nix
  basename = path: last (splitString "/" path);
  src-filter = path: type:
      ext = last (splitString "." path);
      parts = last (splitString "/" path);
      !elem (basename path) [".git" "__pycache__" ".eggs" "_bootstrap_env"] &&
      !startsWith "result" (basename path);
  pip2nix-src = builtins.filterSource src-filter ./.;
    pip2nix = super.pip2nix.override (attrs: rec {
      src = pip2nix-src;
        pkgs.nix
        export NIX_PATH=nixpkgs=${pkgs.path}
        export SSL_CERT_FILE=${pkgs.cacert}/etc/ssl/certs/ca-bundle.crt
  pythonPackagesGenerated = import ./python-packages.nix {
    inherit (pkgs) fetchurl fetchgit fetchhg;
in myPythonPackages.pip2nix

  # Ports of functions for older nix versions
  # https://github.com/NixOS/nixpkgs/blob/0258808f5744ca980b9a1f24fe0b1e6f0fecee9c/lib/lists.nix#L295
  # https://github.com/NixOS/nixpkgs/blob/0258808f5744ca980b9a1f24fe0b1e6f0fecee9c/lib/strings.nix#L257
  # https://github.com/NixOS/nixpkgs/blob/0258808f5744ca980b9a1f24fe0b1e6f0fecee9c/lib/strings.nix#L269
  # https://github.com/NixOS/nixpkgs/blob/8a9f58a375c401b96da862d969f66429def1d118/lib/attrsets.nix#L331
  builtins_fetchTarball = { url, name ? null, sha256 }@attrs:
      inherit (builtins) lessThan nixVersion fetchTarball;
      if lessThan nixVersion "1.12" then
        fetchTarball ({ inherit url; } // (optionalAttrs (name != null) { inherit name; }))
  # fetchurl version that is compatible between all the versions of Nix
  builtins_fetchurl = { url, name ? null, sha256 }@attrs:
      inherit (builtins) lessThan nixVersion fetchurl;
      if lessThan nixVersion "1.12" then
        fetchurl ({ inherit url; } // (optionalAttrs (name != null) { inherit name; }))
        fetchurl attrs;
    { sourcesFile ? if builtins.pathExists ./sources.json then ./sources.json else null

Processing file: ./09/26/synapse-system/vendor/nix/uv2nix/lib/build.nix
{ lib, pyproject-nix, ... }:
  inherit (pyproject-nix.build.lib) renderers;
  inherit (pyproject-nix.lib) pypa;
    nixVersion
  parseGitURL =
    url:
      m1 = match "([^?]+)\\?([^#]+)#(.+)" url;
      m2 = match "([^#]+)#(.+)" url;
        url = elemAt m1 0;
        url = elemAt m2 0;
      throw "Could not parse git url: ${url}";
  # fetchurl doesn't un-escape the name, leaving percent encoding characters in the filename
  srcFilename = url: unquoteURL (baseNameOf url);
        no-binary
        no-binary-package
      unbuildable-packages = intersectLists no-binary-package no-build-package;
      # List of parsed wheels
      fetchurl,
      isURL = source ? url;
      isPath = source ? path; # Path to sdist
        else if no-binary != null && no-binary then
        else if elem package.name no-binary-package then
      gitURL = parseGitURL source.git;
              inherit (gitURL) url;
            // optionalAttrs (versionAtLeast nixVersion "2.4") {
            outPath = "${workspaceRoot + "/${source.path}"}";
            passthru.url = source.path;
          fetchurl {
            url = package.source.url or package.sdist.url;
              whl: whl.url == source.url
            ) (throw "Wheel URL ${source.url} not found in list of wheels: ${package.wheels}") package.wheels;
          fetchurl {
            name = srcFilename wheel.url;
              url
            if selectedWheel ? url then
                fetchurl {
                  name = srcFilename selectedWheel.url;
                  inherit (selectedWheel) url hash;
                lib.warn "wheel url '${selectedWheel.url}' missing hash, falling back to builtins.fetchurl" (
                  builtins.fetchurl {
                    name = srcFilename selectedWheel.url;
                    inherit (selectedWheel) url;
            # Get wheel from local path
            else if selectedWheel ? path then
              (workspaceRoot + "/${source.registry}/${selectedWheel.path}")
              throw "Internal uv2nix error: unsupported selected wheel: ${toJSON selectedWheel}"
    # make sure there is no intersection between no-binary-packages and no-build-packages for current package
      "There is an overlap between packages specified as no-build and no-binary-package in the workspace. That leaves no way to build these packages: "
      format == "wheel" -> no-binary != null -> !no-binary
    ) "Package source for '${package.name}' was derived as sdist, in tool.uv.no-binary is set to true";
    assert assertMsg (format == "wheel" -> !elem package.name no-binary-package)
      "Package source for '${package.name}' was derived as wheel, but was present in tool.uv.no-binary-package";
          optional (hasSuffix ".zip" (src.passthru.url or "")) unzip

Processing file: ./09/26/synapse-system/vendor/nix/uv2nix/lib/lock1.nix
{ pyproject-nix, lib, ... }:
  inherit (pyproject-nix.lib.project) loadUVPyproject;
  inherit (pyproject-nix.lib.pep508) parseMarkers evalMarkers;
  inherit (pyproject-nix.lib.pypa) parseWheelFileName;
  inherit (pyproject-nix.lib) pep440;
    pathExists
      # Lock file as parsed by parseLock
      # PEP-508 environment as returned by pyproject-nix.lib.pep508.mkEnviron
              evalMarkers environ (parseMarkers markers)
    Get relative path for a local package
      or (throw "Not a project path: ${toJSON package.source}");
  parseLock =
      parseOptions =
      # This is so that we only have to parse a marker exactly once even if the marker is repeated
        map (markers: nameValuePair markers (parseMarkers markers)) (
      requires-python = pep440.parseVersionConds requires-python;
      manifest = self.parseManifest manifest;
      package = map (self.parsePackage topLevelMarkers) package;
      options = parseOptions options;
  parseManifest =
  parsePackage =
      parseWheel =
          url ? null,
          path ? null,
        assert (whl ? url) -> (!whl ? filename && !whl ? path);
        assert (whl ? filename) -> (!whl ? url && !whl ? path);
        assert (whl ? path) -> (!whl ? url && !whl ? filename);
        // optionalAttrs (url != null) {
          inherit url;
          file' = parseWheelFileName (baseNameOf url);
          file' = parseWheelFileName filename;
        // optionalAttrs (path != null) {
          inherit path;
          file' = parseWheelFileName (baseNameOf path);
      parseMetadata =
          parseRequires =
              url ? null,
              path ? null,
                url
                path
              marker = if marker != null then parseMarkers marker else null;
              specifier = if specifier != null then pep440.parseVersionConds specifier else null;
          requires-dist = map parseRequires requires-dist;
          requires-dev = mapAttrs (_: map parseRequires) requires-dev;
      parseMarker = marker: topLevelMarkers.${marker} or (parseMarkers marker);
      inherit (pep440) parseVersion;
      parseDependency =
          version' = if version != null then parseVersion version else null;
          marker = if marker != null then parseMarker marker else null;
    ) "A package from uv.lock is called `pkgs` which is a reserved name for the nixpkgs package set";
      version' = pep440.parseVersion version;
      wheels = map parseWheel wheels;
      metadata = parseMetadata metadata;
      # Don't parse resolution-markers.
      dependencies = map parseDependency dependencies;
      optional-dependencies = mapAttrs (_: map parseDependency) optional-dependencies;
      dev-dependencies = mapAttrs (_: map parseDependency) dev-dependencies;
    # Get local packages from lock as an attribute set of pyproject.nix projects.
          if (pathExists (projectRoot + "/pyproject.toml")) then
            # When using submodules with Flakes users need to pass submodules=1 to `nix develop`.
            throwIf (pathExists projectRoot && readDir projectRoot == { })
                This can happen when using Git submodules within a Flake without passing submodules=1 to `nix develop`.
                    internal error: accessed dependencies from pyproject.nix project, not uv.lock
                requires-python = null; # requires-python as parsed by pep621.parseRequiresPython

Processing file: ./09/26/jobs/vendor/nix-task/nix/lib/getTasks.nix
# This file can't import anything as we include the contents of this file
# directly in nix eval --apply arguments (see how this file is included in runner/src/common.ts)
      # see - https://github.com/NixOS/nix/blob/d5e979ab87ab894fde132799dac242780b28bc05/src/libexpr/primops/context.cc#L49
      else if (isAttrSetWithTaskOutput value) then { __type = "taskOutput"; flakeAttributePath = "${currentPath}.${name}.output"; deps = depsWithDerivations "${currentPath}.${name}.output.deps" value.output.deps; }
    else if (isAttrs taskDefinition) && (hasAttr "_nixTaskDontRecurseTasks" taskDefinition) && taskDefinition._nixTaskDontRecurseTasks then []
        flakeAttributePath = currentPath;
        storeDependencies = uniquePredicate (a: b: a != b) (
          (map (pathItem: if (hasAttr "drvPath" pathItem) then pathItem.drvPath else pathItem) taskDefinition.path)
        inherit path;
  # sort by shortest path to task
  sortTasks = a: b: (builtins.stringLength a.flakeAttributePath) < (builtins.stringLength b.flakeAttributePath);

      inherit path;

    nixpkgs.url = "github:meta-introspector/nixpkgs?ref=feature/CRQ-016-nixify";
    flake-utils.url = "github:meta-introspector/flake-utils?ref=feature/CRQ-016-nixify";

            echo "Source path: $src" | tee -a $out/diagnostics/source_analysis.txt
            echo "=== PATH RESOLUTION TEST ===" | tee -a $out/diagnostics/path_resolution.txt
            echo "Attempting to resolve source paths..." | tee -a $out/diagnostics/path_resolution.txt
            # Test different path approaches
            echo "Direct source access:" | tee -a $out/diagnostics/path_resolution.txt
            ls "$src" >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo "✅ Source accessible" || (echo "❌ Source NOT accessible" | tee -a $out/diagnostics/path_resolution.txt && exit 1)
            echo "Bundle path test:" | tee -a $out/diagnostics/path_resolution.txt
            ls "$src/bundle" >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo "✅ Bundle accessible" || (echo "❌ Bundle NOT accessible" | tee -a $out/diagnostics/path_resolution.txt && exit 1)
            echo "Gemini.js path test:" | tee -a $out/diagnostics/path_resolution.txt
            ls "$src/bundle/gemini.js" >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo "✅ gemini.js accessible" || (echo "❌ gemini.js NOT accessible" | tee -a $out/diagnostics/path_resolution.txt && exit 1)
            cat $out/diagnostics/path_resolution.txt | tee -a $out/analysis/summary.txt
                "diagnostics/path_resolution.txt"
          # Build and get result path
          RESULT_PATH=$(nix build --no-link --print-out-paths ${self.packages.${system}.default})
            echo "❌ Test build failed or result path not found"
            echo "  nix build    : Build the test derivation"
            echo "  nix run      : Execute complete test and show report"

  outputs = { self, nixpkgs, flake-utils, gemini-cli, geminiCredsDir ? "${geminiCredsDir}" } @ inputs:
      eachSystem = flake-utils.lib.eachDefaultSystem;
        pkgs = nixpkgs.legacyPackages.${system};
        inherit (nixpkgs) lib;
        flakeNixContent = builtins.readFile (self + "/flake.nix");
            pkgs.curl
          FLAKE_NIX_CONTENT = flakeNixContent; # Pass the content here
            export HOME=$(mktemp -d)
            # Credential files are directly available via extra-sandbox-paths at /creds
              echo "Bundle path: $BUNDLE_PATH"
              echo "Wrapper path: $WRAPPER_PATH"
		"$WRAPPER_PATH" --debug --output-format json --approval-mode yolo --model gemini-2.5-flash --prompt "I am calling you from INSIDE a Nix build derivation! This is real-time telemetry capture. Bundle hash: $BUNDLE_HASH. Time: $(date) Please read flake.nix, and modify it to read @task.md for more instructions. If you cant read flake.nix write a nix package that adds flake.nix, task.md and mission.md to the output. then add a report of the telemetry log via another rust crate and another derivation. we want to create multiple derviations from one task, a whole lattice of them."
              "build_context": "nix_derivation_build_phase",
        nix.settings = {};
            echo "Generated during: nix build --impure --extra-experimental-features impure-derivations"
              echo "Run build first: nix build --impure --extra-experimental-features impure-derivations"

Processing file: ./09/27/7-concepts/ai-workflow/flake.nix
  description = "Consolidated Nix flake for AI/LLM workflows, providing impure LLM job execution and AI derivation functions.";
    nixpkgs.url = "github:meta-introspector/nixpkgs?ref=feature/CRQ-016-nixify";
    flake-utils.url = "github:meta-introspector/flake-utils?ref=feature/CRQ-016-nixify";
  outputs = { self, nixpkgs, flake-utils, ... }:
    flake-utils.lib.eachDefaultSystem (system:
        pkgs = nixpkgs.legacyPackages.${system};
        # --- From impure-llm-job/flake.nix ---
            decryptedPath = builtins.getEnv "LLM_API_KEY_FILE" ""; # Get path from host env
            hasDecrypted = builtins.pathExists decryptedPath;
          buildInputs = [ pkgs.curl pkgs.python3 ]; # Example build inputs
        # --- From response-010-ai-derivation-script/flake.nix ---
          echo "Received NAR paths for inference:"
          while IFS= read -r nar_path; do
            echo "  - $nar_path"
            nix-store --dump $nar_path | nix-nar-unpack $temp_extract_dir
            echo "  Extracted content from $nar_path to $temp_extract_dir"
        # Function to run a flake impurely and produce an AI derivation
        runFlakeForAI = { flakeRef }:
              ({ rustPlatform, lib, openssl, nix }:
                  pname = "input-cli"; # Dummy name, actual name comes from input flake
                  src = flakeRef; # Use the input flake as source
                  cargoLock = "${flakeRef}/Cargo.lock"; # Assuming Cargo.lock is at the workspace root
                  buildInputs = [ openssl nix ];
                  cargoRoot = flakeRef;
                buildInputs = [ pkgs.bash pkgs.nix pkgs.coreutils pkgs.findutils pkgs.gnused pkgs.gnugrep pkgs.gawk pkgs.nix-nar-unpack aiInferenceTool ];
                    buildInputs = [ inputCli pkgs.nix ];
                    dummyNar = pkgs.writeText "dummy-nar-content" "dummy content";
                  echo "$(nix-store --dump $dummyNar)" > $out
              local nar_paths=$(cat $inputCliOutput)
              echo "$nar_paths" | $aiInferenceTool > $ai_output_dir/ai_inference_log.txt
          nativeBuildInputs = [ pkgs.bash pkgs.nix pkgs.curl pkgs.python3 ];
            echo "You can run the impure LLM job with: nix build .#default"
            echo "Example: nix build -f . --arg flakeRef ../response-007-cli-nar-output"

# seed.foaf.nix

    (mkProject { id = "https://github.com/h4ck3rm1k3/sweble-wikitext"; name = "sweble-wikitext"; homepage = "https://github.com/h4ck3rm1k3/sweble-wikitext"; description = "sweble the wikipedia parser wikitext "; makerId = h4ck3rm1k3.id; })
Processing file: ./09/flakes/search-results/flake.nix
# flakes/search-results/flake.nix
    nixpkgs.url = "github:meta-introspector/nixpkgs?ref=feature/CRQ-016-nixify";
    flake-utils.url = "github:meta-introspector/flake-utils?ref=feature/CRQ-016-nixify";
    pickUpNix2.url = "github:meta-introspector/pick-up-nix2?ref=feature/CRQ-016-nixify"; # Assuming pick-up-nix2 is a flake
  outputs = { self, nixpkgs, flake-utils, pickUpNix2, ... }:
    flake-utils.lib.eachDefaultSystem (system:
        pkgs = nixpkgs.legacyPackages.${system};
            # (e.g., via environment variables or sops-nix, not hardcoded here).
                gh search code "${keyword}" --owner "$owner" --json path,repository,text > "$TEMP_DIR/owner_results_$owner.json"
              jq -r '.[] | .path + " " + .repository.name + " " + .text' "$TEMP_DIR/all_search_results.json" | while read -r file_path repo_name snippet;
                mkdir -p "$TEMP_DIR/$repo_name/$(dirname "$file_path")"
                echo "$snippet" > "$TEMP_DIR/$repo_name/$file_path.snippet"
              nix-store --dump "$TEMP_DIR" > "$out"/${(pkgs.lib.replaceStrings [" "] ["-"] keyword)}.nar
              echo "NAR created at $out/${(pkgs.lib.replaceStrings [" "] ["-"] keyword)}.nar"
              gh repo list "${ownerName}" --json name,url,description,owner,isArchived,isFork,createdAt,updatedAt --limit 1000 > "$TEMP_DIR/repos.json"
              nix-store --dump "$TEMP_DIR" > "$out"/repos-${ownerName}.nar
              echo "NAR created at $out/repos-${ownerName}.nar"
            curl # For making HTTP requests to Solana RPC
            BLOCK_INFO=$(curl -s -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
            nix-store --dump "$TEMP_DIR" > "$out"/solana-block-number.nar
            echo "NAR created at $out/solana-block-number.nar"
        # Derivation to create a url2file locator script
        url2fileLocatorScript = { projectRoot }:
          pkgs.writeShellScriptBin "url2file-locate" ''
                echo "Error: GITHUB_URL is not set. Usage: url2file-locate \"https://github.com/owner/repo/blob/branch/path/to/file\"" >&2
            # Extract owner, repo, and path within repo
            # Remove "blob/branch/" part and get the rest of the path
          url2fileLocatorScript = url2fileLocatorScript { projectRoot = pickUpNix2.outPath; }; # Pass pickUpNix2.outPath as projectRoot
              curl # Add curl to devShell for testing
Processing file: ./09/crq-001.foaf.nix
# crq-001.foaf.nix
  problemGoal = "The current log analysis process may not guarantee pure derivation, leading to potential inconsistencies or non-reproducible results. This can hinder debugging, auditing, and the overall reliability of insights derived from logs. For the purpose of this CRQ, **purity** is defined as: a Nix build that reads its inputs from the store and writes its outputs to the store. Goal: Ensure that the log analysis pipeline operates with pure functions and deterministic outputs, making the analysis fully reproducible and verifiable. This aligns with the principles of functional programming and the capabilities offered by Nix for reproducible environments. Specifically, the goal is to create a pure functional system for the collection of Nix store artifacts from GitHub accounts associated with Solana wallets, pointing to IPFS nodes or files that contain Nix flakes of data. All data will be treated as Nix flakes.";
  proposedSolution = "1.  **Refactor Pipeline for Purity:** Identify all stages within the log analysis pipeline (e.g., ingestion, parsing, transformation, aggregation). Refactor each stage to adhere strictly to functional programming principles, where functions produce the same output for the same input and have no side effects. 2.  **Nix Flake-based Data Ingestion:** Implement a pure functional ingestion mechanism to collect Nix store artifacts. This mechanism will interface with GitHub accounts (linked via Solana wallets) to identify repositories containing Nix flakes. Data will be sourced from IPFS nodes or files referenced within these Nix flakes, ensuring all collected data is itself a Nix flake. All data, regardless of its origin, will be treated as a Nix flake, providing a unified and reproducible data format for the entire pipeline. 3.  **Explicit Side-Effect Management:** Isolate and explicitly manage any necessary side-effecting operations (e.g., reading from disk, writing to a database, network calls). These operations should be clearly defined and their impact contained. 4.  **Clear Input-Output Contracts:** Define precise input and output contracts (schemas, types) for each function and stage in the pipeline to ensure data integrity and predictable transformations. 5.  **Nix for Environment Purity:** Utilize Nix to define and manage the entire log analysis environment, including all dependencies, tools, and configurations. This will enforce pure derivation at the environment level, guaranteeing that the analysis always runs in the exact same context. 6.  **Comprehensive Testing:** Develop extensive unit tests for individual pure functions to verify their determinism and correctness. Implement integration tests to ensure that the composition of pure functions within the pipeline maintains overall purity and produces expected results.";
Processing file: ./09/crq-007.foaf.nix
# crq-007.foaf.nix
  proposedSolution = "1.  **Nix-Triggered Execution:** Develop a mechanism where a Nix build is triggered upon specific Solana on-chain events (e.g., payment receipt, transaction finalization, ZKP verification). This Nix build will encapsulate the pure functional Solana code, ensuring a reproducible execution environment. 2.  **Immutable Code and State (CA):** The Solana program/smart contract (CA) will be designed to operate purely functionally, with its logic and associated immutable state stored and referenced via IPFS. This IPFS reference will be integrated into the Nix store, guaranteeing content-addressable and tamper-proof code and state. 3.  **Oracle-like Inputs:** All external inputs required by the Solana code will be treated as \"oracles,\" meaning they are verifiable, immutable, and sourced from trusted, deterministic origins. These inputs will also be integrated into the Nix store, ensuring their immutability and reproducibility during code execution. 4.  **Pure Functional Design Principles:** Adhere strictly to pure functional programming paradigms for the Solana code, eliminating side effects and ensuring that for a given input and immutable state, the output is always the same. Leverage Nix's capabilities to enforce purity at the build and execution environment levels. 5.  **Verifiability and Reproducibility:** The entire system, from event trigger to code execution and output, will be designed for complete verifiability and reproducibility, allowing anyone to independently re-run the Nix build and confirm the Solana program's behavior.";
  justificationImpact = "**Enhanced Security:** Immutable code and state, combined with verifiable inputs, significantly reduce the attack surface and potential for exploits. **Increased Reliability:** Pure functional design ensures deterministic behavior, eliminating common sources of bugs and inconsistencies. **Full Reproducibility:** Leveraging Nix and IPFS guarantees that every execution of the Solana code is fully reproducible, critical for auditing, debugging, and dispute resolution. **Simplified Reasoning:** Developers can reason about the code with greater confidence, as its behavior is predictable and free from hidden side effects. **Decentralization Alignment:** The use of IPFS and Nix store for immutable state and code aligns perfectly with the principles of decentralization and censorship resistance. **Auditability:** The transparent and reproducible nature of the system makes it highly auditable, fostering trust and transparency in on-chain operations.";
Processing file: ./09/crq-008.foaf.nix
# crq-008.foaf.nix
Processing file: ./09/crq-009.foaf.nix
# crq-009.foaf.nix
  proposedSolution = "1.  **Smart Contract Development (Solana):** Develop a robust Solana smart contract to serve as the central coordinator for LLM inference and code authorship payments. The contract will manage user accounts, payment distribution logic, and rate limiting parameters. 2.  **Split Payment Mechanism:** Implement a configurable split payment system within the smart contract. This system will automatically distribute a predefined percentage of the payment to the LLM inference provider and another percentage to the original code author(s) for each successful inference request that utilizes their code. Payment distribution will be transparent and auditable on the Solana blockchain. 3.  **Rate Limiting Implementation:** Integrate a rate-limiting mechanism into the smart contract to control the frequency and/or volume of LLM inference requests per user or per specific resource. This will prevent spamming, ensure fair resource allocation, and protect the system from overload. Rate limits will be configurable and potentially dynamic based on network conditions or governance decisions. 4.  **Code Authorship Attribution:** The smart contract will maintain a registry of code authors and their associated code artifacts (referenced via IPFS/Nix store, as per CRQ-007). Upon successful LLM inference using a specific code artifact, the contract will attribute authorship and trigger the corresponding payment split. 5.  **Event-Driven Coordination:** Leverage Solana's event system to trigger and coordinate various stages of the process: User requests LLM inference. Payment is made to the smart contract. Rate limit check is performed. LLM inference is initiated (off-chain, potentially managed by agents from CRQ-008). Results are returned and verified. Split payment is executed. 6.  **Integration with Pure Functional System (CRQ-007) and Side Effects (CRQ-008):** The smart contract will interact with the pure functional Solana code (CRQ-007) for deterministic execution and leverage the controlled side effect mechanisms (CRQ-008) for external LLM inference calls and secure credential management.";
Processing file: ./09/crq-010.foaf.nix
# crq-010.foaf.nix
  proposedSolution = "1.  **ISO 9000 (Quality Management Systems) Integration:** Establish and document a quality management system (QMS) covering all project activities, from design and development to deployment and maintenance. Implement processes for document control, record keeping, internal audits, and continuous improvement, ensuring adherence to ISO 9000 principles. 2.  **Good Manufacturing Practices (GMP) for Software Development:** Adapt GMP principles to software development, focusing on rigorous control over the software development lifecycle (SDLC). Implement strict version control, change management, testing protocols, and release procedures to ensure software quality and integrity, treating software artifacts as \"manufactured\" products. 3.  **Six Sigma for Process Optimization:** Apply Six Sigma methodologies (DMAIC - Define, Measure, Analyze, Improve, Control) to critical development and operational processes. Identify and eliminate defects, reduce variability, and optimize efficiency in areas such as code generation, deployment pipelines, and incident management. 4.  **ITIL (Information Technology Infrastructure Library) for Service Management:** Adopt ITIL best practices for IT service management, focusing on service strategy, design, transition, operation, and continual service improvement. Implement structured processes for incident management, problem management, change management, and release management to ensure reliable and efficient service delivery. 5.  **Agile Methodologies with Rigor:** Combine the flexibility and responsiveness of Agile (e.g., Scrum, Kanban) with the strict controls of other frameworks. Emphasize iterative development, continuous feedback, and adaptive planning, while ensuring that each iteration adheres to the quality and process standards defined by ISO 9000, GMP, and Six Sigma. 6.  **Extreme Nixism for Ultimate Reproducibility:** Extend the application of Nix principles to their extreme, ensuring that every aspect of the project—from development environments and dependencies to build processes, deployments, and even data artifacts—is purely functional, content-addressable, and fully reproducible within the Nix store. This includes rigorous use of Nix flakes, deterministic builds, and immutable infrastructure principles. 7.  **Integrated Audit and Compliance Framework:** Develop an integrated framework for auditing and compliance that leverages the documentation and traceability provided by all integrated methodologies. Ensure that all processes and artifacts are auditable, providing verifiable proof of adherence to established standards.";
Processing file: ./09/crq-011.foaf.nix
# crq-011.foaf.nix
  proposedSolution = "1.  **Lean 4 Environment Setup (Nix-Managed):** Establish a fully reproducible Lean 4 development environment using Nix, ensuring all dependencies, libraries (e.g., Mathlib), and tools are consistently available. This environment will support the development and verification of Unimath and HoTT-based formalizations. 2.  **Formalization of Core Mathematical Concepts:** Systematically formalize key mathematical concepts and theories relevant to the project (e.g., cryptography, distributed systems, functional programming paradigms) within Lean 4 using Unimath and HoTT principles. Prioritize concepts that underpin the correctness and security of critical project components. 3.  **Proof Development and Verification:** Utilize Lean 4's interactive theorem proving capabilities to construct and formally verify mathematical proofs. Leverage HoTT's interpretation of types as spaces to reason about equivalences and higher-dimensional structures, providing a richer framework for mathematical reasoning. 4.  **Integration with Project Artifacts:** Explore methods to integrate formally verified mathematical results and proofs directly into project artifacts (e.g., as part of Nix flakes, documentation, or even directly influencing code generation). This could involve generating code from verified specifications or using verified properties to validate existing code. 5.  **Training and Expertise Development:** Invest in developing internal expertise in Unimath, HoTT, and Lean 4 to ensure sustainable development and application of these advanced techniques. Foster a culture of formal verification and rigorous mathematical reasoning within the team.";
Processing file: ./09/crq-012.foaf.nix
# crq-012.foaf.nix
  proposedSolution = "1.  **Formal Definition of Nix Pure Derivation in Unimath:** Develop a formal definition of a \"pure derivation\" within the Unimath framework in Lean 4. This definition will capture the essential properties of Nix derivations: content-addressability, immutability, and deterministic output based solely on inputs. This will involve representing Nix store paths, inputs, and outputs as Unimath types. 2.  **Mapping of Nix Properties to Unimath Type Properties:** Systematically map the key properties of Nix pure derivations to corresponding properties of Unimath types: **Reproducibility:** Correlate the deterministic nature of Nix builds to the well-definedness and uniqueness of canonical forms in Unimath types. **Content-Addressability:** Relate the cryptographic hashing of Nix store paths to the identity and equivalence of types in Unimath (e.g., path equality). **Immutability:** Map the immutability of Nix store artifacts to the inherent immutability of mathematical objects and types in Unimath. 3.  **Formal Verification of Derivation Equivalence:** Utilize Lean 4 to formally prove that if two Nix derivations are equivalent (i.e., produce the same output hash), their corresponding Unimath types are also equivalent (e.g., propositionally equal or equivalent via an equivalence). Conversely, prove that distinct Nix derivations correspond to distinct Unimath types. 4.  **Type-Theoretic Representation of Nix Flakes:** Extend the formalization to represent Nix flakes themselves as Unimath types, capturing their structure, inputs, and outputs within the type-theoretic framework. This would allow for formal reasoning about the composition and properties of entire Nix-based projects. 5.  **Implications for Software Verification:** Explore how this formal equivalence can be leveraged for the formal verification of software systems built with Nix. This could involve proving properties about the behavior of Nix derivations directly within Lean 4, providing a new layer of assurance for software correctness.";
Processing file: ./09/crq-013.foaf.nix
# crq-013.foaf.nix
  proposedSolution = "1.  **Vouched Data Collection Framework:** Extend the Nix pure derivation as Unimath type framework (CRQ-012) to formally define and verify data items collected from various sources, including GitHub. Each collected data item will be represented as a Nix pure derivation, whose type can be formally reasoned about in Unimath, providing a strong basis for \"vouching\" for its integrity and provenance. 2.  **GitHub Data Ingestion and Nix Flake Conversion:** Implement a robust ingestion pipeline for GitHub data (e.g., repository metadata, code, commit history). All ingested GitHub data will be converted into Nix flakes, ensuring content-addressability and immutability within the Nix store. 3.  **RocksDB for High-Performance Caching:** Utilize RocksDB, the high-performance key-value store used by Solana, as a local cache for GitHub-related Nix flakes and their associated metadata. This will enable rapid access to frequently used data, reducing reliance on external API calls and improving overall system responsiveness. 4.  **Cache Time and Invalidation Strategy:** Implement a configurable cache invalidation strategy based on a \"cache time\" for GitHub data. Data items in RocksDB will be marked with a timestamp, and older entries will be subject to re-fetching or re-validation from GitHub. This ensures data freshness while minimizing redundant API calls. 5.  **Activity-Based Code Execution Prioritization:** Develop a mechanism to track the \"activity\" of code (e.g., frequency of use, recent commits, associated transactions). Only the \"most active\" code (represented as Nix flakes) will be prioritized for execution, analysis, or further processing, optimizing resource utilization and focusing on relevant data. This can be integrated with the Solana smart contract triggers (CRQ-007) and LLM inference (CRQ-009). 6.  **Formal Vouching Mechanism:** Define a formal process, potentially leveraging ZKPs (Zero-Knowledge Proofs) or cryptographic signatures, to \"vouch\" for the integrity and correctness of collected data items. This vouching process will be tied to the Unimath type representation of the Nix derivations, providing a verifiable guarantee of data quality.";

    inputsFrom = [ self ]; # Inherit inputs from the main flake
      echo "To run verification, evaluate: nix eval .#overallStatus"
      echo "To see detailed results, evaluate: nix eval .#verificationResults"

Processing file: ./09/test.nix
# test.nix - Temporary file for FOAF-OWL verification testing
{ selfPath ? builtins.path { path = ./.; } }:
  common = import ../lib/common-imports.nix {};
  # Mock self for foaf.nix import, as it expects self
  # This is a workaround for direct evaluation outside a flake context
  foafData = import ./foaf.nix { inherit pkgs; self = mockSelf; };
  owlSchema = import ./owl.nix { inherit pkgs lib; };
  verifyFoafOwl = import ./verify-foaf-owl.nix { inherit pkgs lib foafData owlSchema; };

Processing file: ./09/nixify-resource.nix
# nixify-resource.nix
  # Generic function to nixify an external resource
  nixifyResource = { url, strategy }:
        ${pkgs.curl}/bin/curl -L -o $out "${url}"
      # Placeholder for ontology nixification logic
        inherit url;
        path = fetchedResource;
      # Placeholder for code nixification logic
        inherit url;
        path = fetchedResource;
      # Placeholder for document nixification logic
        inherit url;
        path = fetchedResource;
      throw "Unknown nixification strategy: ${strategy}";
  inherit nixifyResource;
Processing file: ./09/lib/common-imports.nix

Processing file: ./09/theory/nix_code_indexer.nix
  indexNixFiles = { path, projectRoot, name ? "nix-file-index" }:
      inherit path projectRoot;
      nativeBuildInputs = [ pkgs.findutils pkgs.nix pkgs.gnused ];
      echo "[" > $out/nix-files.index.json
      find "${path}" -type f -name "*.nix" -print0 | while IFS= read -r -d $'\0' file; do
          file_hash=$(nix hash file --sri "$file")
          relative_path=$(echo "$file" | sed "s|^${projectRoot}/||")
          printf '  {"path": "%s", "hash": "%s"}' "$relative_path" "$file_hash" >> $out/nix-files.index.json
      echo "]" >> $out/nix-files.index.json
Processing file: ./09/theory/n_gram_generator.nix

        path = projectRoot;
    nixFilesIndex;

Processing file: ./09/crq-042.foaf.nix
    This CRQ proposes the development of a new Nix-based import function designed to centralize,
    wrap, cache, and manage all external and internal Nix imports and paths. The function will:
    1.  **Wrap Imports**: Provide a unified interface for all `import` and `fetch` operations.
    3.  **Branch Switching**: Enable dynamic switching of imported flake references to specific branches
        (e.g., `feature/CRQ-016-nixify`) for development, testing, and release management.
    4.  **Documentation & Reporting**: Automatically document import sources, versions, and their
        resolved paths, generating reports for auditability and dependency analysis.
    5.  **Integrity Checks**: Incorporate checks for import integrity, purity, and adherence to
    tags = [ "Nix" "flake" "import-management" "caching" "branching" ];

Processing file: ./10/01/docs/theory/markdown_twin_generator.nix
  common = import ../../../lib/common-imports.nix {};
  # A conceptual function to find and index all .md files in a given path.
    path, # The path to scan for .md files
      inherit path;
      nativeBuildInputs = [ pkgs.findutils pkgs.nix ]; # For `find` and `nix hash` commands
      echo "Indexing .md files in ${path}..." >&2
      # Create a JSON array of objects { path, hash }
      find "${path}" -name "*.md" -print0 | while IFS= read -r -d $'\0' file; do
        relative_path=$(realpath --relative-to="${path}" "$file")
        file_hash=$(nix hash file --base32 "$file") # Get content hash
        echo "  {\"path\": \"${relative_path}\", \"hash\": \"${file_hash}\"}" >> $out/markdown-files.index.json
    markdownFilePath, # Path to the Markdown file in the Nix store
      # Assume a path to a directory containing Markdown files
      # In a real scenario, this would be a specific source directory.
      indexedMDFiles = indexMarkdownFiles { path = markdownSourcePath; };

Processing file: ./10/01/docs/theory/n_gram_generator.nix
  # Function to tokenize a file path string into segments and parts.
  # Example: "/foo/bar/baz.nix" -> [ "foo" "bar" "baz" "nix" ]
  tokenizePath = path: 
      pathSegments = lib.strings.splitString "/" (lib.strings.removePrefix "/" (lib.strings.removeSuffix "/" path));
      splitByDot = lib.flatten (lib.map (segment: lib.strings.splitString "." segment) pathSegments);
  tokenizeNixExpression = nixExpression: # `nixExpression` would be a structured input, not raw string
      # In a real scenario, this would involve a Nix parser (e.g., written in Rust or Haskell)
        "flake" "inputs" "outputs" "nixpkgs" "url" "lib" "pkgs" "stdenv" "mkDerivation"
      path = "/home/user/project/src/my-flake.nix";
      pathTokens = tokenizePath path;
      nixExpressionPlaceholder = {}; # Placeholder for a structured Nix expression
      nixTokens = tokenizeNixExpression nixExpressionPlaceholder;
      allTokens = pathTokens ++ nixTokens;
      pathNGrams = generateNGrams { tokens = pathTokens; inherit nGramLengths; };
      nixNGrams = generateNGrams { tokens = nixTokens; inherit nGramLengths; };
      inherit pathTokens nixTokens allTokens;
      inherit pathNGrams nixNGrams combinedNGrams;

Processing file: ./10/01/docs/theory/nix_code_indexer.nix
  # A conceptual function to find and index all .nix files in a given path.
    path, # The path to scan for .nix files
    projectRoot, # The root path to calculate relative paths against
    name ? "nix-file-index",
      inherit path projectRoot;
      nativeBuildInputs = [ pkgs.findutils pkgs.nix pkgs.gnused ]; # For `find`, `nix hash`, and `sed` commands
      echo "DEBUG: path = ${path}" >&2
      echo "Indexing .nix files in ${path}..." >&2
      # Create a JSON array of objects { path, hash }
      echo "[" > $out/nix-files.index.json
      find "${path}" -type f -name "*.nix" -print0 | while IFS= read -r -d $'\0' file; do
          file_hash=$(nix hash file --sri "$file")
          relative_path=$(echo "$file" | sed "s|^$projectRoot/||")
          printf '  {"path": "%s", "hash": "%s"}' "$relative_path" "$file_hash" >> $out/nix-files.index.json
      echo "]" >> $out/nix-files.index.json
      echo "Nix files indexed in $out/nix-files.index.json" >&2
    nixFileIndex, # Output of indexNixFiles (path to nix-files.index.json)
    # Read the index file and parse it as JSON
    indexedFiles = builtins.fromJSON (builtins.readFile "${nixFileIndex}/nix-files.index.json");
    nixFileIndex, # Output of indexNixFiles (path to nix-files.index.json)
    # The base path from which the index was created, to correctly import files
    indexedFiles = builtins.fromJSON (builtins.readFile "${nixFileIndex}/nix-files.index.json");
    # Create an attribute set where keys are relative paths and values are imported Nix expressions.
    # This is highly conceptual and assumes each .nix file is a valid Nix expression that can be imported.
    importedNixExpressions = lib.listToAttrs (
        name = fileInfo.path; # Use relative path as attribute name
        value = import "${basePath}/${fileInfo.path}"; # Import the Nix expression
    inherit importedNixExpressions;
  # This module can then be imported to quickly access the indexed information.
    nixFileIndex, # Output of indexNixFiles (path to nix-files.index.json)
    name ? "nix-index-cache",
      inherit nixFileIndex;
      echo "# This file is auto-generated by nix_code_indexer.nix" > $out/default.nix
      echo "{ lib, ... }:\n" >> $out/default.nix
      echo "let" >> $out/default.nix
      echo "  indexedFiles = builtins.fromJSON (builtins.readFile \"${nixFileIndex}/nix-files.index.json\");" >> $out/default.nix
      echo "in" >> $out/default.nix
      echo "{\n  rawIndex = indexedFiles;\n  # You could add more processed data here, e.g., grouped by hash, etc.\n}" >> $out/default.nix

Processing file: ./10/01/docs/theory/verifiable_file_topology.nix
  common = import ../../../lib/common-imports.nix {};
    file = null; # Path to the file in the Nix store (string)
    inode = null; # Inode of the file (string, conceptual for Nix store paths)
      url = null; # URL in Git (string)
    nar = {
      path = null; # Path to the NAR file in the Nix store (string, conceptual)
    nix = {
      storePath = null; # Full Nix store path of the file (string)
  # It takes a file (as a Nix store path) and pre-computed impure data as inputs.
    file, # The file (Nix store path) for which to build the topology
    nixHash = pkgs.lib.hashFile "sha256" file;
    nixStorePath = file;
    # Conceptual inode (Nix store paths don't have stable inodes across systems/builds,
    # Conceptual NAR path and hash (would be generated by `nix archive` in an impure step)

      path = conceptualNarPath;
    nix = {
      hash = nixHash;
      storePath = nixStorePath;

Processing file: ./10/01/docs/theory/nix_2gram_indexer_step7.nix
  nixCodeIndexerModule,
  generate2GramIndexStep6Module = import ./nix_2gram_indexer_step6.nix { inherit lib pkgs builtins nixCodeIndexerModule nGramGeneratorModule; };
    value = null; # The 2-gram string (e.g., "flake_inputs")
    uniquePaths = []; # A deduplicated list of file paths where this 2-gram is found
    pathSetHash = null; # A hash representing the unique set of paths (conceptual prime factorization)
    projectRoot, # The root path of the project to index
    name ? "nix-2gram-index",
        # Get unique file paths where this 2-gram is used
        # Sort paths for consistent hashing of the set
        # Hash the sorted unique paths to get a unique identifier for the set
        pathSetHash = builtins.hashString "sha256" (lib.strings.concatStringsSep ";" sortedUniquePaths);
        inherit pathSetHash;

Processing file: ./10/09/hackathon/nar-bridge-flake/flake.nix
  description = "A flake to bridge between Nix store paths and NAR archives.";
    nixpkgs.url = "github:meta-introspector/nixpkgs?ref=feature/CRQ-016-nixify";
  outputs = { self, nixpkgs } @ inputs:
      pkgs = nixpkgs.legacyPackages.aarch64-linux;
      # Function to create a NAR archive from a given store path
      # Usage: nar-bridge-flake.lib.createNar { name = "my-archive"; path = /nix/store/...; }
      lib.createNar = { name, path }:
        pkgs.runCommand "${name}-nar" {
          inherit path;
          buildInputs = [ pkgs.nix ];
          echo "DEBUG: Running ${pkgs.nix}/bin/nix nar pack for path: $path"
          echo "DEBUG: Output file: $out/${name}.nar"
          ${pkgs.nix}/bin/nix nar pack "$path" > "$out/${name}.nar" || { echo "ERROR: nix nar pack failed!"; exit 1; }
      lib.restoreNar = { name, narFile }:
        pkgs.runCommand "${name}-restored" {
          inherit narFile;
          buildInputs = [ pkgs.nix-nar-unpack ]; # Use the dedicated nix-nar-unpack package
          mkdir -p $out/restored-content
          nix-nar-unpack --file "$narFile" --to "$out/restored-content" || { echo "ERROR: nix-nar-unpack failed!"; exit 1; }
          echo "$out/restored-content" > "$out/restored-path"
Processing file: ./10/09/hackathon/nar-bridge-flake/test.nix
  pkgs = import <nixpkgs> { system = "aarch64-linux"; };
  # Get the nar-bridge-flake outputs
  narBridgeFlake = (builtins.getFlake (toString ./.)).outputs;

    path = dummyDerivation;
  # 3. Use restoreNar to restore the archive
  restoredPath = narBridgeFlake.lib.restoreNar {
    name = "restored-dummy";
    narFile = archivedNar;
  # 4. Verify the restored path
  testResult = pkgs.runCommand "test-nar-bridge" {
    inherit restoredPath;
    # Read the actual restored path from the file
    ACTUAL_RESTORED_PATH=$(cat "$restoredPath/restored-path")

Processing file: ./10/09/nix-grep-regexes.nix
  narRegexes = [
    "nix-nar"
    "nar pack"
    "nar unpack"
    "nix-store --import"
    "nix-store --export"
    "nix archive"
    "nix store dump-path"
  grepPatterns = pkgs.lib.concatStringsSep "|" narRegexes;
  # Find all .nix files in the current directory and subdirectories
  grepResult = pkgs.runCommand "nix-nar-grep-results" {
    find "$src" -name "*.nix" -print0 | xargs -0 grep -E "${grepPatterns}" > "$out/grep-results.txt" || true # Allow grep to exit with 1 if no matches

{ lib, pkgs, flakeSource, inputFlakes, processFlakes, outputFlakes }:
  "    nixpkgs.url = \"${flakeSource}\";"
  (pkgs.lib.concatStringsSep "\n" (pkgs.lib.map (f: "    input-${lib.strings.sanitizeDerivationName f}.url = \"${f}\";") inputFlakes))
  (pkgs.lib.concatStringsSep "\n" (pkgs.lib.map (f: "    process-${lib.strings.sanitizeDerivationName f}.url = \"${f}\";") processFlakes))
  (pkgs.lib.concatStringsSep "\n" (pkgs.lib.map (f: "    output-${lib.strings.sanitizeDerivationName f}.url = \"${f}\";") outputFlakes))
  "  outputs = inputs@{ self, nixpkgs, ... }:"
  "      pkgs = import nixpkgs { system = \"x86_64-linux\"; };"
  "        echo \"This derived flake represents a partial application with holes.\" >> \"$out\"\n"

Processing file: ./10/10/lib-llm-nix/lib/task-phases.nix
{ lib, pkgs, flakeSource, inputFlakes, processFlakes, outputFlakes }:
  # Import the flake template generator
  generateFlakeTemplateString = import ./flake-template-generator.nix {
    inherit lib pkgs flakeSource inputFlakes processFlakes outputFlakes;
  # Write the generated flake template string to a temporary file for the scripts to use
  initialDerivedFlakeContentFile = pkgs.writeText "initial-derived-flake.nix" generateFlakeTemplateString;
  # Plan Phase: Generate a script that creates and builds the derived flake, including LLM review
  planDerivation = pkgs.runCommand "plan-${lib.strings.sanitizeDerivationName flakeSource}" {
  } "${llmReviewScript} ${flakeSource} ${initialDerivedFlakeContentFile} ${pkgs.gemini-cli}/bin/gemini";
  # Commit Phase: Displays the generated flake.nix for the derived flake
  commitDerivation = pkgs.runCommand "commit-${lib.strings.sanitizeDerivationName flakeSource}" {} "${commitScript} ${flakeSource} ${planDerivation}";
  runDerivation = pkgs.runCommand "run-${lib.strings.sanitizeDerivationName flakeSource}" {} "${runScript} ${flakeSource} ${planDerivation}";
  # Eval Phase: Builds and tests the derived flake directly
  evalDerivation = pkgs.runCommand "eval-${lib.strings.sanitizeDerivationName flakeSource}" {} "${evalScript} ${flakeSource} ${initialDerivedFlakeContentFile}";

  lib.map generateTask flakeSources

Processing file: ./10/10/nix2/lib/first-reflection.nix
      # Placeholder for actual extraction logic (e.g., functions that read files and parse them)
      extractFlakeCommands = flakePath:
          flakeContent = builtins.readFile flakePath;
          runCommandMatches = lib.strings.match "pkgs\\.runCommand \"[^\"]*\" \{[^}]*\} \"([^\"]*)\"" flakeContent;
          writeShellScriptMatches = lib.strings.match "pkgs\\.writeShellScript \"[^\"]*\" \"([^\"]*)\"" flakeContent;
          # Heuristic to find flake input URLs
          urlMatches = lib.strings.match "url = \"([^\"]*)\"" flakeContent;
          systemMatches = lib.strings.match "system = \"([^\"]*)\"" flakeContent;
          urls = lib.map (m: m.captures.c1) urlMatches;
        { inherit commands urls systems; };
          urlMatches = lib.strings.match "url = ([^\n]*)" gitmodulesContent;
        lib.map (m: m.captures.c1) urlMatches;
      description = "Functions to normalize extracted commands (e.g., removing whitespace, standardizing arguments, resolving paths) to identify true uniqueness.";
    nixWrapperDefinition = {
    # Defines a central registry where all defined Nix wrappers are stored and made accessible.
      findDirectCommandUsage = { command, path }:
          echo "Searching for direct usage of '${command}' in '${path}'" > "$out"
          grep -r "${command}" "${path}" >> "$out" || true
    flakeValidation = {
      description = "Validation rules specific to Nix flakes, such as URL and system constraints.";
      validateFlakeUrls = urls:
          invalidUrls = lib.filter (url: ! (lib.strings.hasPrefix "github:meta-introspector" url)) urls;
      validateUrlsWithPrefixes = { urls, allowedPrefixes }:
          invalidUrls = lib.filter (url: !(lib.any (prefix: lib.strings.hasPrefix prefix url) allowedPrefixes)) urls;
        urlPrefixValidationReport,
        flakeHashes,
          This is a sample of intermediate results from the Nix flake identity principle enforcement.
          ${builtins.toJSON flakeHashes}
          Has Invalid URLs: ${if urlPrefixValidationReport.hasInvalidUrls then "Yes" else "No"}
          Invalid URLs: ${builtins.toJSON urlPrefixValidationReport.invalidUrls}

Processing file: ./10/10/nix2/createContext.nix
  path,
  utils = import ./lib/utils.nix { inherit lib pkgs; };
  # Convert path to string explicitly
  pathStr = builtins.toString path;
  # Get .nix filenames in the current path
  nixFilenames = utils.getNixFiles path;
  # Construct full paths for the .nix files
  nixFilesRaw = map (filename: pathStr + "/" + filename) nixFilenames;
  nixFiles = builtins.deepSeq nixFilesRaw nixFilesRaw;
  inherit nixFiles;

Processing file: ./10/10/nix-url-extractor.nix
{ lib, pkgs, firstReflection, flakePaths, gitmodulesPaths }:
  # Extract all URLs from flakes
    lib.map (flakePath: (firstReflection.identityPrincipleSpec.rawCommandExtraction.extractFlakeCommands flakePath).urls) flakePaths
  # Function to parse a GitHub URL into its components
  parseGithubUrl = url:
      # Regex to match github:owner/repo?ref=branch&dir=path or https://github.com/owner/repo/tree/branch/path
      githubMatch = lib.strings.match "github:([^/]+)/([^?#]+)(\\?ref=([^&]+))?(&dir=(.*))?" url;
      httpsMatch = lib.strings.match "https://github.com/([^/]+)/([^/]+)/tree/([^/]+)/(.*)" url;
      inherit url;
      inherit url;
      owner = null; repo = null; ref = null; dir = null; inherit url;
  parsedUrls = lib.map parseGithubUrl allUniqueGithubUrls;
  # Function to generate the content of a repo.nix file
  generateRepoNixContent = { owner, repo, ref, dir, url }:
        # Original URL: ${url}
        flakeNix = "${repoPath}/flake.nix";
        flakeNix = if builtins.pathExists flakeNix then flakeNix else null;
        gitmodules = if builtins.pathExists gitmodules then gitmodules else null;
        url = "${url}"; # Store the original URL
  # Instructions for generating repo.nix files
  repoFileInstructions = lib.map (parsedUrl: {
    path = "10/10/github/${parsedUrl.owner}/${parsedUrl.repo}.nix"; # This path needs to be relative to the root of the project
    content = generateRepoNixContent parsedUrl;
  }) parsedUrls;
  urls = allUniqueGithubUrls;
  inherit parsedUrls repoFileInstructions;

  extractFlakeFeatures = { flakePath, extractedCommands, extractedUrls, extractedSystems }:
      # This would involve parsing the flake's structure, inputs, outputs, etc.
  # Abstract function to calculate a similarity score between two flakes
  calculateFlakeSimilarity = { flakeFeatures1, flakeFeatures2 }:
  # Abstract function to find the 'n' closest neighbors for a given flake
    # This would involve calculating similarity with all other flakes and sorting
    []; # Return a list of closest flake identifiers
  # Abstract function to generate a proof of relation between a flake and its neighbors

Processing file: ./10/10/context.nix
  # Path to the .gitmodules file for pick-up-nix context
  pickUpNixGitmodulesPath = ../../context/pick-up-nix/.gitmodules;
  # Function to parse a .gitmodules file
  parseGitmodules = gitmodulesPath:
      submoduleSections = lib.strings.match ''^\[submodule "([^"]+)"\]\n\s*path = ([^\n]+)\n\s*url = ([^\n]+)(\n\s*branch = ([^\n]+))?'' content;
          path = lib.head (lib.tail match.captures);
          url = lib.head (lib.tail (lib.tail match.captures));
        { inherit name path url branch; };
  pickUpNixSubmodules = if builtins.pathExists pickUpNixGitmodulesPath then parseGitmodules pickUpNixGitmodulesPath else [];
  streamOfRandomSubmodules = if builtins.pathExists streamOfRandomGitmodulesPath then parseGitmodules streamOfRandomGitmodulesPath else [];
Processing file: ./10/10/bootstrap-context.nix
      import ./bootstrap-context/part1.nix

Processing file: ./10/10/export-nar.nix
{ pkgs ? import <nixpkgs> {} }:
  nix2-src = pkgs.lib.sources.cleanSource ./nix2;
  get-nix-file-list = nix2-src + "/get-nix-file-list.nix";
pkgs.runCommand "nix-file-list-nar" {} ''
  ${pkgs.nix}/bin/nix eval --raw -f ${get-nix-file-list} > nix-file-list.json
  # Add the JSON file to the store
  store_path=$(${pkgs.nix}/bin/nix-store --add nix-file-list.json)
  # Dump the store path to a NAR file
  ${pkgs.nix}/bin/nix-store --dump $store_path > $out
Processing file: ./crq-parser.nix
{ pkgs ? import <nixpkgs> {} }:
  nixFiles = import ./nix-indexer.nix { inherit pkgs; };
  # Filter for CRQ .foaf.nix files
  foafCrqFiles = pkgs.lib.filter (p: pkgs.lib.hasSuffix ".foaf.nix" p && pkgs.lib.hasPrefix "09/crq-" p) nixFiles;
  mdCrqFiles = pkgs.lib.filter (p: pkgs.lib.hasSuffix ".md" p && pkgs.lib.hasPrefix "docs/crqs/CRQ_" p) nixFiles;
  # Function to get CRQ ID from .foaf.nix files (e.g., "crq-001.foaf.nix" -> "CRQ-001")
  getFoafCrqId = path: let
    baseName = builtins.baseNameOf path; # e.g., "crq-001.foaf.nix"
  getMdCrqId = path: let
    baseName = builtins.baseNameOf path; # e.g., "CRQ_041_Colosseum_Mirror_Flake.md"

    url = "https://example.com"; # Replace with the actual spec website URL

    path = self;
    name = "nix-files-index";

    inherit lib pkgs builtins nixOntologyRepo nixpkgs nixCodeIndexerModule;

    path = nixOntologyRepo;
    name = "ontology-urls";
  inherit nixToOwlOntology;
  inherit nixToOwlOntologyModule;

      url = "./parser"; # Reference the local parser flake
        nixpkgs.follows = "nixpkgs";
        flake-utils.follows = "flake-utils";
      url = "./base"; # Reference the local base flake
        nixpkgs.follows = "nixpkgs";
        flake-utils.follows = "flake-utils";
      url = "./layer1"; # Reference the local layer1 flake
        nixpkgs.follows = "nixpkgs";
        flake-utils.follows = "flake-utils";
  outputs = { self, nixpkgs, flake-utils, parser, base, layer1 }:
      pkgs = nixpkgs.legacyPackages.${system};
      # Expose a test output for the parser function
      tests.parserTest = {
        value = parser.lib.parseCrqFilename "CRQ_001_Log_Analysis_Pure_Derivation.md";
        meta.description = "Root flake for the CRQ search lattice, aggregating sub-flakes for testing.";
      } "echo 'CRQ Search Lattice flake built successfully!' > $out";

        DESCRIPTION = "Layer 1 flake for CRQ search lattice.";
          flakeName = "layer1";
          nixpkgsRef = nixpkgs.rev or "";
          flakeUtilsRef = flake-utils.rev or "";

          buildInputs = [ pkgs.nix ];

          nixpkgsPath = nixpkgs; # Pass nixpkgs as a build input

          buildInputs = [ pkgs.nix pkgs.jq ];
          buildInputs = [ pkgs.nix ]; # Only nix is needed here to build the run-crq-check-script
          RUN_CRQ_CHECK_SCRIPT=$(${pkgs.nix}/bin/nix build .#packages.${system}.run-crq-check-script --no-link --print-out-paths)
            --nixpkgs-path ${nixpkgs} \

  # Function to run nixpkgs-fmt --check on a list of Nix file paths.
  runNixFmtCheck = nixFiles: pkgs.runCommand "nixpkgs-fmt-check" {
    inherit nixFiles;
    inherit (pkgs) nixpkgs-fmt;
    echo "Running nixpkgs-fmt --check on Nix files..."
    for nixFile in $nixFiles; do
      echo "  - Checking formatting: $nixFile"
      ${nixpkgs-fmt}/bin/nixpkgs-fmt --check $nixFile
  # Function to run statix check on a list of Nix file paths.
  runStatixCheck = nixFiles: pkgs.runCommand "statix-check" {
    inherit nixFiles;
    for nixFile in $nixFiles; do
      echo "  - Checking static analysis: $nixFile"
      ${statix}/bin/statix check $nixFile

Processing file: ./lib/generate-project-nix/processEntry.nix
# lib/generate-project-nix/processEntry.nix
# It decides whether to recurse into a directory, evaluate a .nix file, or ignore the entry.
# Returns the evaluated content (if a .nix file) or the result of a recursive call (if a directory),
path:
    fullPath = path + "/" + name;
  else if type == "regular" && lib.strings.hasSuffix ".nix" name && name != "project.nix" && name != "flake.nix" then
    # Evaluate .nix files using the helper function
      evaluated = (import (builtins.fetchTarball "github:meta-introspector/time-2025?ref=feature/foaf&dir=lib/generate-project-nix/evaluateNixFile.nix")) { file = fullPath; inherit errors types builtins; };
    import (builtins.fetchTarball "github:meta-introspector/time-2025?ref=feature/foaf&dir=10/04/lib/error-isolation.nix") { evalResult = evaluated; file = fullPath; inherit builtins errors types; }

              paths = [ self.packages.${system}.orchestratorApp ];
          node2nix-src.packages.${system}.default # Add node2nix for JavaScript dependency management
          nurl.packages.${system}.default # Add nurl to the development shell

Processing file: ./theory/nix-indexer-pipeline.nix
# theory/nix-indexer-pipeline.nix
# from Nix paths and metadata.
  rawIndexJson = builtins.readFile ../nix-files.index.json;
  tokenizePath = path:
      normalizedPath = lib.replaceStrings ["/" "-" "."] [" " " " " "] path;
        ! (lib.elem s ["nix" "json" "md" "sh" "txt" "d" "flake" "lib" "src" "docs" "theory" "meme" "memestorm" "tiktok" "001" "09" "24" "25" "26" "jobs" "vendor" "nixpkgs" "github" "meta" "introspector" "streamofrandom" "2025" "pick" "up" "cli" "gemini" "today" "ai" "ml" "zk" "ops" "flakes" "external" "build" "step" "build_step" "common" "imports" "debug" "utils" "test" "utils" "log" "analyzer" "review2" "rungemini" "run_task" "nix_custom_attrs_test" "test_custom_attrs" "setup_gemini_context" "leech" "lattice" "24d" "monster" "group" "71" "vibe" "crqs" "blade" "instance" "blades" "d17"])
  # Extract all tokens from all indexed file paths
  allTokens = lib.flatten (lib.map (file: tokenizePath file.path) indexedFiles);

Processing file: ./theory/hackathon-mycology-workflow-puml/flake.nix
  description = "Nix flake for the Hackathon Mycology Workflow PlantUML diagram.";
    nixpkgs.url = "github:meta-introspector/nixpkgs?ref=feature/CRQ-016-nixify";
    flake-utils.url = "github:meta-introspector/flake-utils?ref=feature/CRQ-016-nixify";
  outputs = { self, nixpkgs, flake-utils }:
    flake-utils.lib.eachDefaultSystem (system:
        pkgs = nixpkgs.legacyPackages.${system};
        LAYER_DUALITY_2_46 = "46 interwoven layers of binary distinctions (${monster_genome_data.Duality})"; #
        # These would typically be inputs from other flakes, but for this puml, we'll mock them.
        # The original content of hackathon_mycology_workflow.puml.nix
    Container(cwm_verification, "CWM/FOAF Verification Module", "Nix Derivation (cwm.nix)", "Semantic validation of Agent Identity (foaf:Agent) and compliance with OWL schema before deployment (CRQ-002, CRQ-030)").
    Container(mycelial_strain_storage, "Spore Vial Storage", "Nix Store/IPFS", "Stores artifact portfolios and gene codes as Content-Addressable Spore Vials (Nix flakes/derivations)").
  Use_Case(process_nar, "Process Content-Addressable NARs")
  Use_Case(store_meme, "Store Mined Memes in Lattice")
happy --> store_meme
bashful --> process_nar
doc --> process_nar
doc --> store_meme
mine_meme .> process_nar : <<uses>>
store_meme .> process_nar : <<uses>>
analyze_oeis .> store_meme : <<extends>>
Processing file: ./uncommitted.nix
   ./project.nix ./test-commit-check.nix ./uncommitted.nix
Processing file: ./grep-inputs.nix
{ pkgs ? import <nixpkgs> {}, src ? builtins.toString ./. } :
  # Find all .nix files in the current directory and subdirectories
    find "${src}" -name "*.nix" -print0 | xargs -0 grep -E "${grepPatterns}" > "$out/grep-results.txt" || true # Allow grep to run even if no matches are found
Processing file: ./wrap_context.nix
{ lib, path, name }:
      # Helper function to parse a .gitmodules file
      parseGitmodules = gitmodulesPath:
            (builtins.fromJSON "[ \"^\\[submodule \\\"([^\\\"]+)\\\"\\]\\n\\\\s*path = ([^\\\\n]+)\\\\n\\\\s*url = ([^\\\\n]+)(?:\\\\n\\\\s*branch = ([^\\\\n]+))?\"]")
              url = lib.elemAt match 2;
            { inherit name subPath url branch; };
      # Get .nix files in the current path
      nixFiles = lib.filter (
        file: builtins.match ".*\\.nix" file != null
      ) (builtins.attrNames (builtins.readDir path));
      # Get submodules in the current path
      gitmodulesPath = path + "/.gitmodules";
      submodulesRaw = if builtins.pathExists gitmodulesPath then parseGitmodules gitmodulesPath else [];
            value = wrap_context { inherit lib; path = path + "/" + sub.subPath; inherit (sub) name; };
      files = map (file: path + "/" + file) nixFiles;
Processing file: ./content.nix
  pkgs = import <nixpkgs> {};
  wrap_context_def = import ./wrap_context.nix;
  pickupnix_context = wrap_context_def.wrap_context {
    paths = [ /data/data/com.termux.nix/files/home/pick-up-nix2 ];
    name = "pickupnix";
    paths = [ /data/data/com.termux.nix/files/home/pick-up-nix2/source/github/meta-introspector/streamofrandom ];
    paths = [ /data/data/com.termux.nix/files/home/pick-up-nix2/source/github/meta-introspector/streamofrandom/2025 ];
  pickupnix = pickupnix_context;
